Payscribe Landing Page (Next.js) — LLM Instructions

Purpose
- Marketing site + content/blog/press pages.
- Includes an admin dashboard for managing blog posts, press items, and contact submissions.

Tech stack
- Next.js (App Router)
- React
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- MongoDB (admin/content backend)
- Cloudinary (image upload/storage)

Runtime requirements
- Node.js: 24.x (see `package.json` engines and `.nvmrc`)

How to run (local)
- Install: `npm install`
- Dev: `npm run dev` (http://localhost:3000)
- Build: `npm run build`
- Start: `npm run start`
- Lint: `npm run lint`

Key directories
- `src/app/`: App Router pages (public + admin)
- `src/app/api/`: API routes (admin + public)
- `public/`: static assets
- `scripts/`: utility scripts (e.g. admin creation/testing)

Important pages (App Router)
- `/` → `src/app/page.tsx`
- `/blog` and `/blog/[slug]`
- `/press`
- `/contact-us`, `/book-a-demo`, `/faqs`, `/privacy-policy`, `/terms-and-conditions`, `/use-case`, etc.
- Admin:
  - `/admin/login` → login
  - `/admin` → dashboard
  - `/admin/blog`, `/admin/press`, `/admin/contact`

API overview
- Public:
  - `POST /api/contact`
  - `GET /api/press`
  - `GET /api/blog`
  - `GET /api/blog/[slug]`
- Admin (cookie/JWT auth):
  - `POST /api/admin/auth/login`, `GET /api/admin/auth/me`, `POST /api/admin/auth/logout`
  - `GET /api/admin/dashboard`
  - Blog CRUD: `/api/admin/blog`, `/api/admin/blog/[id]`, publish/unpublish
  - Press CRUD: `/api/admin/press`, `/api/admin/press/[id]`
  - Contact: `/api/admin/contact`, `/api/admin/contact/[id]`
  - Upload: `/api/admin/upload/image`, `/api/admin/upload/image/delete`

For route details and UI integration points
- See `documentation.md`

Redirects & images
- Redirects are configured in `next.config.ts` (e.g. `/invoice` → `/invoicing`)
- Remote images allowed from Cloudinary and Medium (see `next.config.ts`)

Security / privacy (very important)
- Do NOT read, print, or commit secrets.
- This repo contains `.env` patterns in `.gitignore`, but local `.env` files may still exist.
- Prefer using environment variables configured in your runtime (Vercel/local shell) and, if needed, create a sanitized `.env.example`.
- Never include credentials (Mongo URI, JWT secrets, Cloudinary keys) in code changes, logs, or documentation output.

What to optimize for when making changes
- Keep public pages fast (avoid heavy client-side JS when possible).
- Maintain SEO metadata and accessible UI.
- Prefer server components by default; use client components only when necessary.
